frozen_components: BERT MLM head (for entity linking, repurposed with aligned entity vectors)
architecture.summary: E-BERT aligns Wikipedia2Vec entity embedding vectors into BERT's wordpiece vector space via a learned linear transformation, then injects aligned entity vectors into BERT's input layer without modifying the encoder. Two strategies are explored: E-BERT-concat concatenates entity IDs (via aligned vectors) with wordpiece tokens as a single input sequence; E-BERT-replace substitutes entity surface forms with aligned entity vectors. The aligned vectors flow through BERT's standard transformer stack unchanged, leveraging BERT's pretrained MLM head for entity-centric tasks.
trained_components: linear alignment transformation (Wikipedia2Vec → BERT wordpiece space)
task-specific heads (relation classification softmax, entity linking MLM)

biblio.title: E-BERT: Efficient-Yet-Effective Entity Embeddings for BERT

claims.statement: E-BERT-concat sets a new state of the art on LAMA with Hits@1 of 36.2 (BASE model)

claims.dataset: LAMA-UHN (hardened benchmark with unhelpful entity names filtered)
claims.statement: E-BERT-replace drops by less than 1% between original LAMA and LAMA-UHN, while BERT drops by about 8%

claims.dataset: FewRel (preprocessed subset used by Zhang et al. 2019)
claims.statement: E-BERT-concat achieves F1 of 88.38 on relation classification test set
E-BERT-MLM achieves micro F1 of 85.0 on AIDA-B (test set) entity linking task
The original BERT model is overly reliant on the surface form of entity names (e.g., guessing that someone with an Italian-sounding name speaks Italian), and E-BERT mitigates this problem
E-BERT requires no expensive further pretraining of the BERT encoder, in contrast to ERNIE and KnowBert
Training Wikipedia2Vec took 6 hours on 32 CPUs, and fitting the linear transformation is negligible in cost
E-BERT lacks entity vectors for 1% of LAMA questions and 2% of FewRel sentences; in these cases the system falls back to standard BERT behavior
